name: P1 Home Loans
description_short: P1 Home Loans is an Arizona-based mortgage lender offering home purchase and refinance loans across six states with online applications and rate quotes.
description_long:
P1 Home Loans, officially Preferred One Home Loans, LLC, is a mortgage lender headquartered in Arizona that has built its business around direct client relationships and streamlined digital processes. The company was founded with a focus on understanding clients' home financing goals through dedicated service and transparent communication. Today, P1 operates as a licensed mortgage lender across Arizona, California, Connecticut, Florida, Ohio, and Texas, positioning itself as a full-service mortgage provider in a competitive market.

The company offers two primary loan products: home purchase mortgages for first-time and repeat homebuyers, and refinance loans marketed with emphasis on competitive rates and low fees. P1 provides interactive mortgage calculators on its website to help borrowers evaluate different scenarios and financing options. Their application process is conducted entirely online through a secure platform that takes approximately 12 minutes to complete. Once submitted, applicants receive contact from the lender regarding pre-approval status. The company also offers pre-qualification tools to help prospective borrowers determine their eligibility before formal application.

P1 differentiates itself through personalized service from named loan officers and emphasis on efficient remote processing. Customer testimonials highlight specific team members (Tim Hevesy, Alex) and praise the company for responsiveness, quick turnaround times, and rate savings. The company markets its ability to serve clients across multiple states without requiring in-person office visits, as evidenced by a customer review from Panama City, Florida working with their Arizona office. This remote-first approach combined with individual loan officer attention appears to be their competitive positioning.

P1 Home Loans operates as a legitimate licensed mortgage lender with demonstrated customer activity and positive reviews. However, the website lacks specific information about loan types (FHA, VA, conventional), interest rate ranges, fees, credit requirements, or loan terms. The company provides no third-party verification, licensing numbers, or NMLS details on the website itself. Borrowers should independently verify licensing status and obtain detailed Loan Estimate forms before committing, as with any mortgage lender.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Licensed to operate in six states (Arizona, California, Connecticut, Florida, Ohio, Texas)
Online secure application takes only 12 minutes to complete
Interactive mortgage calculators available to evaluate different loan scenarios
Remote processing allows service to borrowers across multiple states without office visits
Named loan officers assigned to applications based on customer testimonials
Offers both purchase and refinance loan programs
Pre-qualification tools available before formal application submission
cons: Website lacks specific information about loan types (FHA, VA, conventional, jumbo, ARM details)
No published interest rates, fee schedules, or APR ranges disclosed online
No NMLS number, licensing verification, or regulatory details provided on website
Limited transparency about credit score requirements, debt-to-income limits, or approval criteria
Customer reviews are exclusively from Arizona and Florida, limited geographic diversity of testimonials
best_for: Homebuyers and refinancers in Arizona, California, Connecticut, Florida, Ohio, or Texas seeking remote loan processing
Borrowers who prefer personalized service from individual loan officers over automated lenders
Applicants comfortable with online-only application and approval workflow
diagnosis: P1 Home Loans is best suited for borrowers in their six licensed states who value personalized remote service and want to avoid traditional bank branch visits. The primary caveat is lack of online price transparency—borrowers must submit applications or quote requests to learn specific rates, terms, and fees, which prevents easy rate shopping comparison.
services: Loan officer consultation and application support
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 7141 N 51st Ave Suite D1 and D2, Glendale, AZ 85301
